data.title: As a new project member, I am prompted to install missing dependencies before onboarding proceeds so I don't encounter cryptic errors
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a dependency pre-scan phase before any onboarding questions that checks for required dependencies (bash >= 4.0, GNU coreutils, git) and optional dependencies (ast-grep, semgrep)
**Why**: Users currently encounter cryptic bash 3 errors or coreutils failures mid-onboarding with no guidance on how to fix them
**Scope**:
- IN: Check bash version via /bin/bash --version (not PATH-resolved bash), check coreutils, check git, present single brew install command for missing required deps, offer optional deps separately, timeout for restricted networks
- OUT: Non-Homebrew platform support (documented prerequisite per 91f5-0aec), automatic installation without user confirmation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, onboarding checks for bash >= 4.0 (via /bin/bash --version), GNU coreutils, and git before asking any questions
  ← Satisfies: "Before asking any questions, onboarding checks for required dependencies"
- When this story is complete, if required deps are missing, a single brew install command is presented and onboarding pauses until the user confirms
  ← Satisfies: "presents a single brew install command and pauses until the user confirms"
- When this story is complete, optional deps (ast-grep, semgrep) are offered separately and never block progress, with a timeout to prevent hanging
  ← Satisfies: "Optional deps are offered separately and never block progress"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must handle Homebrew unavailability with explicit out-of-scope messaging
- [Reliability] Install commands need timeout for air-gapped/proxy environments
